identity.name: The Princess Who Slept Long
identity.story: Pricked a finger on Dame Spindle's wheel back when it still spun, and slept the full hundred years the story promised while Old Thorn grew a wall nobody could cross. The arch finally came in at its full height, roses and all, and she walked out through it same as it had been waiting on her the whole time. Sets an alarm now. Has not once used the snooze.

relationships.kind: the hedge that walled her in for a hundred years and finally let her out; she's decided to forgive it, mostly for the roses
kept the wheel that started it, three locks deep now — the two of them still haven't managed to be in the same room
swapped notes once: one of them never slept soundly again, the other's still deciding if she's proud she can

behavior.label: the Princess's hundred years, all slept at once

child.label: waking on the first try, still faintly surprised the ceiling isn't stone
thanking the hedge, out loud, roses in hand and the thanks costing her nothing she isn't glad to spend

children.label: walking to the arch that finally let her through
telling Old Thorn the roses were worth the wait, growls and all

child.label: walking the lane wide of anything that spins
sleeping one ordinary night's worth, alarm set, snooze untouched
